metric_value: Desktop business drivers; PC/NC/NetPC choice dilemma; Microsoft/Intel approach; Network Computing approach; where each architecture is best suited.
Expanded internal communications (intranets); expanded external communications (extranets); JIT supply chain; globalization eliminating distances.
Analyze -> Decide -> Transact -> Report framework taught as the new desktop workflow model.
Kastner's implicit framing: Wintel PC remains the default for general enterprise desktop use; NC/NetPC fit limited use cases.
Network Computer suited for narrow task-worker / kiosk use cases; unlikely to displace fat-client PC for knowledge workers.
NetPC as Microsoft/Intel compromise between PC and NC unlikely to establish a durable category.

Wintel PC remained the dominant enterprise desktop architecture through the 2000s; was later complemented (not replaced) by thin-client/VDI, Chromebooks, and web-SaaS — vindicating Kastner's 1997 framing over the decade.
Network Computer failed as a commercial category; Oracle/Sun's NC effort wound down by 2000. Kastner prediction verified.
NetPC failed to establish a durable commercial category; Microsoft and Intel quietly abandoned NetPC branding by 1999. Kastner prediction verified.
Intranets framed as a primary desktop investment driver — consistent with actual enterprise intranet adoption 1997-2003.
